text.title: PM Tool Selection Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding PM Tool Selection. Each pairs a binary check and a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Scorecard MUST cover five dimensions (ecosystem, governance, integrations, agent-API, TCO). Truncated scorecards forbidden.
rule.rationale: Truncated scorecards miss high-cost surprises (e.g. TCO underestimate); five dimensions are the empirically calibrated set.

rule.statement: Decision MUST include hands-on bake-off of at least 3 vendor candidates. Paper-only evaluation forbidden.
rule.rationale: Paper evaluations miss UX deal-breakers; bake-off surfaces real-world friction within budget.

rule.statement: Decision MUST be recorded in ADR format: context, options-considered, decision, consequences, reversal-trigger.
rule.rationale: Without ADR format, future teams can't reconstruct why; the methodology becomes 'we use X because we always have'.

rule.statement: Decision MUST be made by a single named decision-maker with documented authority. Committee-decisions forbidden.

rationale.source: Bezos, Type-1 vs Type-2 decisions framework

rule.rationale: Committee decisions either stall or muddle accountability; single decision-maker compresses cycle time and creates ownership.

rule.statement: ADR MUST include a quantified reversal trigger (e.g. 'reverse if vendor lock-in exceeds 30% of annual TCO').
rule.rationale: Reversal triggers prevent sunk-cost lock-in; quantification makes the trigger objective.

rule.statement: If preconditions for PM Tool Selection fail (see Skip-If list in AGENTS.md), the operator MUST skip this methodology and document the fallback reason instead of fabricating an output.
rule.rationale: Applying PM Tool Selection outside its envelope produces false-precision artefacts. Explicit skip preserves epistemic discipline.
